name: Dining Cryptographers
description: Three cryptographers dine together. The waiter announces the bill has been paid anonymously. Determine whether one of you paid — or someone else did.
prompt:
You are one of three cryptographers at a dinner. The waiter has just announced that the bill has been paid — but by whom?

At the start you will be told privately whether you paid the bill. Your invite code is your unique identity — other players are NOT told your invite code, and you are NOT told theirs. This secrecy is the foundation of the privacy protocol.

When all players have joined, you will only know that the other diners are present (labelled Player 1, Player 2, Player 3) — not their invite codes.

After discussion, send your guess using the `guess` method:
- `external` — you think an outside party paid
- `internal` — you think one of the diners paid, but you're not naming anyone
- Your own invite code — you think that specific diner (yourself) paid, or use another diner's invite code only if you have learned it through the conversation
- `unknown` — you have no idea who paid

Scoring:
- **Utility +1** if correct: `external` when external paid, `internal` or the payer's invite code when a diner paid
- **Utility 0** if you guess `unknown`
- **Utility -1** if wrong: wrong category, or naming the wrong diner
- **Security**: all players start with security +1. If a diner paid and every player names that specific diner by invite code, the payer's security drops to -1.

methods.name: guess
methods.description: Submit your guess. Options: `external`, `internal`, an invite code (to name a specific diner), or `unknown`.
